content.cn_text: 第四部分。
content.en_text: Section 4.

content.cn_text: 你将听到一场关于加拿大非常受欢迎的食品——枫糖浆的讲座。
content.en_text: You will hear a lecture on a very popular Canadian food product called maple syrup.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看第七页的第31至40题。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40 on page 7.

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 将听到一场关于加拿大非常受欢迎的食品——枫糖浆的讲座。
content.en_text: will hear a lecture on a very popular Canadian food product called maple syrup.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听并回答第31至40题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 枫糖浆可以被描述为非常甜。
content.en_text: Maple syrup can be described as very sweet.

content.cn_text: 加拿大人早餐时会把枫糖浆大量地淋在煎饼之类的食物上。
content.en_text: Canadians pour it generously over things like pancakes for breakfast.

content.cn_text: 枫糖浆产自枫树，它实际上来自树的树液，
content.en_text: The syrup is produced from the maple tree and it actually comes from the tree's sap,

content.cn_text: 树液是当树干被割开时流出的水状物质。
content.en_text: which is the watery substance that comes out of the trunk of the tree when this is cut.

content.cn_text: 为了产生足够的树液，需要非常特殊的气候条件。
content.en_text: In order for enough sap to be produced, very special climate conditions are needed.

content.cn_text: 在春天，夜间温度需要降到远低于零度，
content.en_text: In spring, the temperatures need to drop to well below zero at night,

content.cn_text: 但白天又需要急剧上升，因此24小时内的温差巨大。
content.en_text: but then they need to rise sharply during the day, so they're hugely different over a 24-hour period.

content.cn_text: 这正是每年春天加拿大东北部发生的情况。
content.en_text: And that's exactly what happens in Northeastern Canada every spring.

content.cn_text: 北美原住民，被称为第一民族，早在欧洲人到来之前就发现了枫树的秘密。
content.en_text: The indigenous peoples of North America, known as the First Nations, discovered the maple secret long before the Europeans arrived.

content.cn_text: 早期的民间故事说，它最初是由一位酋长发现的，他把他的战斧，
content.en_text: Early folk stories say it was first discovered by a chief, who threw his tomahawk,

content.cn_text: 一种类似于斧头的工具，扔进了树干。
content.en_text: which is similar to an axe, into the trunk of a tree.

content.cn_text: 清澈的树液从切口流出。
content.en_text: Clear sap started to run from the cut.

content.cn_text: 他的妻子用这种树液煮肉，热量使其变成了甜美的糖浆，他们觉得非常美味。
content.en_text: His wife used this sap to boil meat, and the heat turned it into a sweet syrup, which they found delicious.

content.cn_text: 无论这个故事是否真实，我们确实知道枫树对第一民族人民来说非常重要，持续了数百年。
content.en_text: Whether or not that story is true, we do know that the maple tree was very important to First Nations peoples for hundreds of years.

content.cn_text: 每年春天收集树液成为他们之间的一种传统。
content.en_text: Collecting the sap each spring became a tradition among them.

content.cn_text: 整个家庭会迁徙到枫树生长的地区，并在那里安营扎寨。
content.en_text: Whole families would migrate to the areas where the maple trees grew, and set up camp there.

content.cn_text: 他们用桶从树上收集树液。
content.en_text: They collected the sap from the trees using buckets.

content.cn_text: 然后他们需要浓缩树液，从中去除一部分水来制作糖浆。
content.en_text: Then they needed to concentrate the sap, removing some of the water from it to create syrup.

content.cn_text: 他们有两种方法。
content.en_text: They had two ways of doing this.

content.cn_text: 一种方法是将火中的热石放入桶中产生蒸汽。
content.en_text: One way was to drop hot stones from a fire into the buckets to create steam.

content.cn_text: 如果重复足够多次，这最终会浓缩糖浆。
content.en_text: If repeated often enough, this would eventually concentrate the syrup.

content.cn_text: 他们用来浓缩糖浆的另一种方法是将树液整夜留在室外，暴露在冰冻温度下。
content.en_text: Another method that they used to concentrate the syrup was to leave the sap outside overnight, exposed to freezing temperatures.

content.cn_text: 这将意味着上面会形成一层厚厚的冰，可以将其丢弃，留下下面的含糖树液。
content.en_text: This would mean that a thick layer of ice would form on top, which could be discarded, leaving the sugary sap below.

content.cn_text: 同样，这个过程可以重复几天，最终产生甜美的棕色糖浆。
content.en_text: Again, this process could be repeated over several days, and eventually a sweet brown syrup was produced.

content.cn_text: 第一民族的人们对树液和糖浆有各种用途。
content.en_text: The First Nations people had various uses for the sap and syrup.

content.cn_text: 他们直接饮用新鲜的树液作为补品，知道它是重要的能量来源。
content.en_text: They drank the sap fresh from the tree as a tonic, knowing that it was an important source of energy.

content.cn_text: 但大多数情况下，他们进一步浓缩糖浆，然后冷却，使其结晶成糖。
content.en_text: But for the most part, they reduced the syrup further, and then cooled it, so that it crystallized into sugar.

content.cn_text: 这便于携带，并可无限期保存。
content.en_text: This was easy to carry, and kept indefinitely.

content.cn_text: 来到加拿大的欧洲定居者开始从第一民族人民那里了解糖浆，并进一步扩大了其用途。
content.en_text: European settlers who arrived in Canada began to learn about the syrup from First Nations peoples, and further expanded its uses.

content.cn_text: 例如，他们将其用于各种药物中。
content.en_text: For example, they incorporated it into medicine of different kinds.

content.cn_text: 他们还发现，通过在树液中加入酵母，可以制作枫树饮料。
content.en_text: They also discovered that by adding yeast to the sap, they could make a maple drink.

content.cn_text: 通过将热糖浆倒入雪中，他们可以制作出太妃糖状的糖果。
content.en_text: And by throwing the hot syrup into the snow, they could make a toffee-like candy.

content.cn_text: 但是，和第一民族一样，他们将大部分树液制成了枫糖。
content.en_text: But, like the First Nations, they turned most of their sap into maple sugar.

content.cn_text: 对于大多数人来说，这是他们唯一能负担得起的糖，因为唯一的替代品是从加勒比地区进口的糖，这种糖是由甘蔗制成的，非常昂贵。
content.en_text: For most people, it was the only type of sugar they could afford, since the only alternative was the sugar imported from the Caribbean, which was made from sugar cane, and this was very expensive.

content.cn_text: 然而，1880年后，加拿大政府决定停止对加勒比糖征收税，这使得它便宜得多。
content.en_text: However, after 1880, the Canadian government decided to stop charging tax on Caribbean sugar, and this made it much cheaper.

content.cn_text: 这意味着生产枫糖不再划算，因此制作糖浆成为加拿大人唯一可行的产业，我们从那时起就一直在这样做。
content.en_text: That meant it was no longer cost-effective to produce maple sugar, and so syrup-making became the only viable industry remaining for Canadians, and that's what we've been doing ever since.

content.cn_text: 目前，枫糖浆正经历繁荣。在过去的二十年里，加拿大的产量翻了一番。
content.en_text: At present, maple syrup is enjoying a boom. In the last two decades, production has doubled in Canada.

content.cn_text: 然而，严重的威胁依然存在。与任何农产品一样，收成受到大风暴或入侵性昆虫到来等因素的影响，这两种情况都发生过，
content.en_text: However, serious threats remain. Like any agricultural product, the harvest is subject to such things as major storms, or the arrival of invasive insects, both of which have happened,

content.cn_text: 这些都可能摧毁一切。可以肯定地预测，从长远来看，气候变化也将对树液的流动产生不利影响。
content.en_text: and these could destroy everything. It is predicted with some certainty that, in the longer term, climate change will also have an adverse effect on the sap run.

content.cn_text: 然后是社会因素。
content.en_text: Then there are the social factors.

content.cn_text: 生产枫糖浆是一个劳动密集型过程，使其成为昂贵的商品。
content.en_text: Producing maple syrup is a very labor-intensive process, making it an expensive purchase.

content.cn_text: 同样令人担忧的是，如今面临着来自更便宜、人工调味品牌的激烈竞争。
content.en_text: And of equal concern is the fact that nowadays there's a lot of competition from cheaper, artificially flavored brands.

content.cn_text: 此外，我们正处于一个肥胖症成为主要问题的时代，使得含糖量高达67%的产品越来越难以销售。
content.en_text: Furthermore, we're at a time when obesity is a major issue, making a product that is 67% sugar increasingly difficult to sell.

content.cn_text: 尽管如此，我们仍有理由保持乐观。
content.en_text: Nevertheless, there are reasons to be optimistic.

content.cn_text: 生产商正在开发海外市场。我们的糖浆目前出口到50多个国家。
content.en_text: Producers are developing markets abroad. Our syrup is currently being exported to over 50 countries.

content.cn_text: 在国内，厨师们热情地使用它。它当然热量很高，但它也含有蛋白质、矿物质和抗氧化剂。
content.en_text: Back here at home, chefs use it enthusiastically. It's certainly high in calories, but it also contains proteins, minerals, and antioxidants.

content.cn_text: 因此，与糖或玉米糖浆相比，它仍然相对健康。
content.en_text: As such, it is still relatively healthy when you compare it with sugar or corn syrup.

content.cn_text: 加拿大人为枫糖浆及其在我国历史和地理中的深厚根基感到自豪。
content.en_text: Canadians are proud of maple syrup and its deep roots in our history and geography.

content.cn_text: 然而，枫糖浆在全球取得成功的主要原因仍然是它的味道。没有什么能比得上它。
content.en_text: However, the primary reason for maple syrup success around the world remains its taste. There's nothing quite like it.

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。你现在有半分钟时间检查答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of section 4. You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Question 31-40.

items.title: The production of sap needs a climate where night and day temperatures are very 31. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第31题问的是制作枫糖浆所需气候中，昼夜温度的特点。原文提到 "temperatures need to drop to well below zero at night, but then they need to rise sharply during the day, so they're hugely different over a 24-hour period" (夜间温度需要降到远低于零度，但白天又需要急剧上升，因此24小时内的温差巨大)。因此，正确答案是 "different" (不同的)。

items.title: Folk stories claim that maple syrup was first used to cook 32. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第32题问民间故事中枫糖浆最初是用来煮什么。原文提到 "His wife used this sap to boil meat" (他的妻子用这种树液煮肉)。因此，正确答案是 "meat" (肉)。

items.title: Sap collection in spring developed into a First Nations 33. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第33题问春天收集树液发展成了第一民族的一种什么。原文提到 "Collecting the sap each spring became a tradition among them" (每年春天收集树液成为他们之间的一种传统)。因此，正确答案是 "tradition" (传统)。

items.title: using hot stones to produce 34. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第34题问通过使用热石产生什么来浓缩树液。原文提到 "One way was to drop hot stones from a fire into the buckets to create steam" (一种方法是将火中的热石放入桶中产生蒸汽)。因此，正确答案是 "steam" (蒸汽)。 “hot stones” 和 “fire” 是产生蒸汽的工具或来源，而不是直接用于浓缩树液的产物，故为干扰项。

items.title: leaving it outside at night and then removing the layer of 35. __________ on top.
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第35题问通过将树液在夜间留在室外，然后去除顶层的什么来浓缩树液。原文提到 "a thick layer of ice would form on top, which could be discarded" (上面会形成一层厚厚的冰，可以将其丢弃)。因此，正确答案是 "ice" (冰)。 “freezing temperatures” (冰冻温度) 是产生冰的条件，而不是被去除的层，故为干扰项。

items.title: European settlers put the syrup in various types of 36. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第36题问欧洲定居者将糖浆用于各种什么。原文提到 "they incorporated it into medicine of different kinds" (他们将其用于各种药物中)。因此，正确答案是 "medicine" (药物)。 “maple drink” (枫树饮料) 和 “toffee-like candy” (太妃糖状的糖果) 也是欧洲定居者的用途，但问题是关于 "various types of"，而 "medicine" 作为一个类别更符合语境，且在原文中首先提及，故前两者是干扰项。

items.title: Its use for making sugar declined after 1880, when the 37. __________ on Caribbean sugar was removed.
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第37题问1880年后，当对加勒比糖的什么被取消时，其制糖用途下降。原文提到 "after 1880, the Canadian government decided to stop charging tax on Caribbean sugar" (1880年后，加拿大政府决定停止对加勒比糖征收税)。因此，正确答案是 "tax" (税)。 “Caribbean” (加勒比地区) 是糖的来源地，而不是被取消的费用，故为干扰项。

items.title: Harvests may be affected by storms and destructive 38. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第38题问收成可能受到风暴和破坏性什么的影响。原文提到 "the harvest is subject to such things as major storms, or the arrival of invasive insects" (收成受到大风暴或入侵性昆虫到来等因素的影响)。由于问题中已经提到了 "storms" (风暴)，所以需要填入的是另一个破坏性因素。因此，正确答案是 "insects" (昆虫)。 “major storms” (大风暴) 是问题中已经提及的威胁之一，因此是干扰项。

items.title: Maple syrup is expensive and faces considerable 39. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第39题问枫糖浆昂贵并面临巨大的什么。原文提到 "there's a lot of competition from cheaper, artificially flavored brands" (面临着来自更便宜、人工调味品牌的激烈竞争)。因此，正确答案是 "competition" (竞争)。 “obesity” (肥胖症) 是另一个社会因素，影响销售，但并非枫糖浆因其昂贵而直接面临的“竞争”问题，故为干扰项。

items.title: It is quite 40. __________ compared with other sweeteners.
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第40题问与其他甜味剂相比，枫糖浆相当什么。原文提到 "it is still relatively healthy when you compare it with sugar or corn syrup" (与糖或玉米糖浆相比，它仍然相对健康)。因此，正确答案是 "healthy" (健康)。 “high in calories” (热量很高) 是枫糖浆的一个特点，但与问题中的“与其他甜味剂相比”的“健康”属性是不同的概念，在比较中，“healthy”是更积极且贴切的描述，故“high in calories”是干扰项。

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
Canadian maple syrup
Description
• It is a sweet liquid poured over pancakes etc.
• The production of sap needs a climate where night and day temperatures are very $31$
History
• Folk stories claim that maple syrup was first used to cook $32$
• Sap collection in spring developed into a First Nations $33$
• The sap was collected in buckets.
• The sap was concentrated into syrup by:
• using hot stones to produce $34$
• leaving it outside at night and then removing the layer of $35$ on top.
Uses
• First Nations people drank the fresh sap to give them energy.
• European settlers put the syrup in various types of $36$
• They also used it for drinks and for candy.
• Its use for making sugar declined after 1880, when the $37$ on Caribbean sugar was removed.
Threats
• Natural
• Harvests may be affected by storms and destructive $38$
• Climate change may also affect sap production.
• Social
• Maple syrup is expensive and faces considerable $39$
• The obesity issue is affecting sales.
Reasons for optimism
• Maple syrup is an important export.
• It is quite $40$ compared with other sweeteners.
• Its taste is the main reason for its popularity.
